summary: - Writing CSS for Markdown involves targeting standard HTML elements, and classless CSS can be effective here - There's interest in using classless CSS frameworks (like those at classless.de or github.com/dbohdan/classless-css) to style Markdown content with minimal CSS - Some discussion on the redundancy of targeting both `` and `` elements, suggesting that focusing on `` is sufficient - Interest in tools or methods to dynamically showcase Markdown rendering alongside its source, possibly with interactive elements like sliders - The goal is to create clean, bloat-free websites that scale well, with an emphasis on minimal CSS and semantic HTML <｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>
